title: Phishing Email Detected
description: Email from spoofed sender 'noreply@secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru' delivered to user inbox. Subject: 'Urgent: Verify Your Account'. Attachment: invoice_march.html

title: DNS Query to Known Phishing Domain
description: Host LAPTOP-JS04 issued DNS query for secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru. Domain flagged in threat intelligence feed (TI-FEED-002). Query resolved to 185.220.101.47.

title: Suspicious URL Clicked
description: User navigated to hxxps://secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru/login?ref=corp after opening phishing email attachment. Browser: Chrome 122. URL matches phishing domain IOC.

title: Credential Submission to Unknown Host
description: POST request detected to hxxps://secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru/submit containing form data consistent with username/password fields. Possible credential harvesting in progress.

title: Phishing Email Detected
description: Same phishing campaign email delivered to secondary recipient. Sender domain secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru. Subject identical. Email not yet opened.

title: DNS Query to Known Phishing Domain
description: Host DESKTOP-MW11 issued DNS query for secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru. Same phishing domain as evt-1002. User opened the email and followed embedded link.

title: Suspicious URL Clicked
description: User mwilliams navigated to hxxps://secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru/login?ref=corp. Destination IP 185.220.101.47 matches known C2 for phishing kit.

title: Phishing Email Detected
description: Third recipient received same phishing email from secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru. Email quarantined automatically before delivery by spam filter.

title: DNS Query to Known Phishing Domain
description: LAPTOP-RC07 DNS query for secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru despite quarantine. Possible alternate delivery vector (personal email on corp device). Query blocked by DNS sinkhole.

title: Credential Submission to Unknown Host
description: POST to hxxps://secure-login.bank-verify[.]ru/submit from LAPTOP-RC07. Despite sinkhole being applied, traffic routed through VPN split-tunnel. Credentials likely exfiltrated.
